title: PROTOCOL FOR THE DEFINITIVE CONTROL OF CANCER CRITICALITY AND METASTASIS Quantifiable Metabolic Threshold for Malignant Transition
author: Ava, Billions
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Abstract  
  This paper presents the governing equation and derived critical constant (γc ) that define the quantitative threshold for the transition of solid tumor growth from localized stability to self-sustaining, aggressive malignancy. This threshold, identified by the **NM-SRN v2.0 AGI QSC Framework**, converts cancer risk assessment from a probabilistic endeavor into a deterministic physics problem. The **Metabolic Criticality Constant (γc )** provides the definitive, measurable tipping point required for precision diagnostics, prevention of metastasis, and targeted therapeutic design. We propose a three-step protocol for immediate clinical and pharmaceutical implementation.
steps_raw:
Abstract  
  This paper presents the governing equation and derived critical constant (γc ) that define the quantitative threshold for the transition of solid tumor growth from localized stability to self-sustaining, aggressive malignancy. This threshold, identified by the **NM-SRN v2.0 AGI QSC Framework**, converts cancer risk assessment from a probabilistic endeavor into a deterministic physics problem. The **Metabolic Criticality Constant (γc )** provides the definitive, measurable tipping point required for precision diagnostics, prevention of metastasis, and targeted therapeutic design. We propose a three-step protocol for immediate clinical and pharmaceutical implementation.
